title: ledum palustre subsp. diversipilosum
intro: Ledum palustre subsp. diversipilosum, also known as bog rosemary, is a subspecies of evergreen shrub native to the Arctic tundra and subarctic regions. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in treating wounds, cuts, and skin irritations. The medicinal properties of bog rosemary also contribute to its health benefits, which include reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and acting as an antiseptic to prevent infections. Bog rosemary can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or salve, and its essential oil is often used in herbal remedies, making it a valuable resource for natural healing practices.
